title: Umbrella issue: bottleneck C Extension Analysis Report
author: devdanzin
body:
Below is part of an analysis report created by [cext-review-toolkit](https://github.com/devdanzin/cext-review-toolkit). It contains segfaults under OOM, memory leaks on error paths (~80 KB per invalid `partition`/`argpartition` call), a per-call reference leak in `rankdata` on non-C-contiguous arrays, systemic missing NULL checks in INIT/INIT_ONE macros affecting ~46 generated functions, and unchecked malloc in `mm_new`/`mm_new_nan`. The full report is available at https://gist.github.com/devdanzin/b9ce99c1971afa418b6a24ca575c67c5.

This is meant as an umbrella issue from which specific sub issues can be created.

# bottleneck C Extension Analysis Report
## Findings by Priority
### FIX

- #519
- #520
- #521
- #522
- #523
- #524
- #525
- #526
- #527

### CONSIDER

- #528
- #529
- #530
- #531
- #532

----

## Summary Table

| # | Finding | Classification | Confidence |
|---|---------|---------------|------------|
| 1-2 | #519 `move_median` MEMORY_ERR segfault | **FIX** | HIGH |
| 3-4 | #520 `partition`/`argpartition` ~80 KB leak on error | **FIX** | HIGH |
| 5 | #521 `nonreducer_axis` Ravel reference leak + NULL | **FIX** | HIGH |
| 6 | #522 `nonreducer_axis` RUNTIME_ERR without return | **FIX** | HIGH |
| 7-8 | #523 `rankdata`/`nanrankdata` unchecked ArgSort+EMPTY | **FIX** | HIGH |
| 9 | #524 `push` unchecked PyArray_Copy | **FIX** | HIGH |
| 10-11 | #525 INIT/INIT_ONE macro unchecked EMPTY (~46 functions) | **FIX** | HIGH |
| 12-14 | #526 `mm_new`/`mm_new_nan` unchecked malloc + mm_free on NULL | **FIX** | HIGH |
| 15 | #527 Missing `pystr_kth` check | **FIX** | HIGH |
| 16 | #528 `iterators.h` Ravel unchecked | CONSIDER | HIGH |
| 17 | #529 BUFFER_NEW unchecked malloc | CONSIDER | HIGH |
| 18 | #530 Module leak on init failure | CONSIDER | MEDIUM |
| 19 | #531 Exception clobbering (~15 sites) | CONSIDER | MEDIUM |
| 20 | #532 Free-threading global state | CONSIDER | HIGH |

comments.author: rgommers
comments.body:
Hi @devdanzin, I've seen your work on `cext-review-toolkit` before and think it's quite useful, so I appreciate this issue and the linked bug reports. That said, I recommend on other projects to not open many issues at once, it might get you banned given that many OSS projects struggle with "AI slop" spam.

I assume you validated all the linked bug reports, and they're real and need fixing?

comments.author: devdanzin
comments.body:
Thank you, and point taken. I've been contacting maintainers and asking them how I should proceed, in this case @neutrinoceros said it'd be OK to open the issues (as we did similarly for h5py before).

I've read and analyzed all the bug reports, but validating is a strong word for the cursory confirmations I did. E.g. I haven't tried applying the suggested fixes. I think it's likely there will be false positives, hopefully few (both I and the agents expend some time weeding those out) . 

I can't say they all need fixing, there's probably some issues that are intended behavior or not important enough. But I did my best to add context, explain why it was considered a bug, and offer fix suggestions.

comments.author: rgommers
comments.body: Ah great, thanks for the context.

comments.author: neutrinoceros
comments.body: In h5py we've seen no false positives, so I green lighted @devdanzin to do more of the same here (thank you !). I'll mark all other tickets as sub-issues to this one.

comments.author: neutrinoceros
comments.body: also note for @rgommers on my methodology addressing these; I'll triage them manually over the next few days, marking the ones I'm confident I can confirm as bugs, and fixing anything I can as I go. If all goes well, I expect the easy, more numerous ones, will get a patch early, and then whatever remains can get more in depth analysis and discussion.

comments.author: rgommers
comments.body: Sounds great, thanks for working on this!

comments.author: neutrinoceros
comments.body: So far, so good. I confirmed 5/5 reports I studied, and opened PRs with the exact suggestions included therein. As I'm already doing the job of a reviewer, I feel like it should be okay if I "self"-merge these, though, before I do, would you be okay with this approach @rgommers ?

comments.author: rgommers
comments.body: @neutrinoceros sure, that sounds fine to me. I have browsed the most recent open PRs, and those all look good to me. In case you want review on a specific one, please ping me.
